root_text.an2.11:1.6: ‘kāyaduccaritassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, vacīduccaritassa pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, manoduccaritassa pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañcā’ti.
root_text.an2.11:1.7: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti, vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti, manoduccaritaṁ pahāya manosucaritaṁ bhāveti, suddhaṁ attānaṁ pariharati.
root_text.an2.11:2.2: Tatra, bhikkhave, yamidaṁ bhāvanābalaṁ sekhānametaṁ balaṁ.
root_text.an2.11:2.3: Sekhañhi so, bhikkhave, balaṁ āgamma rāgaṁ pajahati, dosaṁ pajahati, mohaṁ pajahati.
root_text.an2.11:2.4: Rāgaṁ pahāya, dosaṁ pahāya, mohaṁ pahāya yaṁ akusalaṁ na taṁ karoti, yaṁ pāpaṁ na taṁ sevati.
root_text.an2.12:1.6: ‘kāyaduccaritassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, vacīduccaritassa pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, manoduccaritassa pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañcā’ti.
root_text.an2.12:1.7: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti, vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti, manoduccaritaṁ pahāya manosucaritaṁ bhāveti, suddhaṁ attānaṁ pariharati.
root_text.an2.12:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vosaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.an2.12:2.8: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vosaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.an2.13:1.6: ‘kāyaduccaritassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, vacīduccaritassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañca, manoduccaritassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañcā’ti.
root_text.an2.13:1.7: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti, vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti, manoduccaritaṁ pahāya manosucaritaṁ bhāveti, suddhaṁ attānaṁ pariharati.
root_text.an2.13:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi, vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an2.13:2.3: Vitakkavicārānaṁ vūpasamā ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ avitakkaṁ avicāraṁ samādhijaṁ pītisukhaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an2.13:2.4: Pītiyā ca virāgā upekkhako ca viharati sato ca sampajāno, sukhañca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti, yaṁ taṁ ariyā ācikkhanti: ‘upekkhako satimā sukhavihārī’ti tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an2.13:2.5: Sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an2.14:1.4: Imā kho, bhikkhave, dve tathāgatassa dhammadesanā”ti.
root_text.an2.15:1.1: “Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe āpanno ca bhikkhu codako ca bhikkhu na sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an2.15:1.2: ‘dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca na phāsuṁ viharissantī’ti.
root_text.an2.15:1.3: Yasmiñca kho, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe āpanno ca bhikkhu codako ca bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an2.15:1.4: ‘na dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca phāsuṁ viharissantī’ti.
root_text.an2.15:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, āpanno bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati?
root_text.an2.15:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, āpanno bhikkhu iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an2.15:2.3: ‘ahaṁ kho akusalaṁ āpanno kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:2.4: Maṁ so bhikkhu addasa akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:2.5: No ce ahaṁ akusalaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena, na maṁ so bhikkhu passeyya akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:2.6: Yasmā ca kho, ahaṁ akusalaṁ āpanno kañcideva desaṁ kāyena, tasmā maṁ so bhikkhu addasa akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:2.7: Disvā ca pana maṁ so bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena anattamano ahosi.
root_text.an2.15:2.8: Anattamano samāno anattamanavacanaṁ maṁ so bhikkhu avaca.
root_text.an2.15:2.9: Anattamanavacanāhaṁ tena bhikkhunā vutto samāno anattamano ahosiṁ.
root_text.an2.15:2.11: Iti mameva tattha accayo accagamā suṅkadāyakaṁva bhaṇḍasmin’ti.
root_text.an2.15:2.12: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, āpanno bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati.
root_text.an2.15:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, codako bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati?
root_text.an2.15:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, codako bhikkhu iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an2.15:3.3: ‘ayaṁ kho bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpanno kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:3.4: Ahaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ addasaṁ akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:3.5: No ce ayaṁ bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpajjeyya kañcideva desaṁ kāyena, nāhaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ passeyyaṁ akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:3.6: Yasmā ca kho, ayaṁ bhikkhu akusalaṁ āpanno kañcideva desaṁ kāyena, tasmā ahaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ addasaṁ akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena.
root_text.an2.15:3.7: Disvā ca panāhaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ akusalaṁ āpajjamānaṁ kañcideva desaṁ kāyena anattamano ahosiṁ.
root_text.an2.15:3.8: Anattamano samāno anattamanavacanāhaṁ imaṁ bhikkhuṁ avacaṁ.
root_text.an2.15:3.9: Anattamanavacanāyaṁ bhikkhu mayā vutto samāno anattamano ahosi.
root_text.an2.15:3.11: Iti mameva tattha accayo accagamā suṅkadāyakaṁva bhaṇḍasmin’ti.
root_text.an2.15:3.12: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, codako bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati.
root_text.an2.15:4.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe āpanno ca bhikkhu codako ca bhikkhu na sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca na phāsuṁ viharissantīti.
root_text.an2.15:4.2: Yasmiñca kho, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe āpanno ca bhikkhu codako ca bhikkhu sādhukaṁ attanāva attānaṁ paccavekkhati tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ na dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca phāsuṁ viharissantī”ti.
root_text.an2.16:1.1: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an2.16:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an2.16:1.3: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an2.16:1.4: “Adhammacariyāvisamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an2.16:2.1: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an2.16:2.2: “Dhammacariyāsamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an2.16:3.2: Seyyathāpi, bho gotama, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ bhotā gotamena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.an2.16:3.3: Esāhaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.an2.16:3.4: Upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
root_text.an2.17:1.1: Atha kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an2.17:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an2.17:1.3: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an2.17:1.5: Evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an2.17:1.6: “Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an2.17:1.8: Evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an2.17:1.9: “Na kho ahaṁ imassa bhoto gotamassa saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.an2.17:1.10: Sādhu me bhavaṁ gotamo tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathā ahaṁ imassa bhoto gotamassa saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājāneyyan”ti.
root_text.an2.17:1.11: “Tena hi, brāhmaṇa, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an2.17:1.12: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an2.17:2.1: “Idha, brāhmaṇa, ekaccassa kāyaduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti kāyasucaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.17:2.2: vacīduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti vacīsucaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.17:2.3: manoduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti manosucaritaṁ.
root_text.an2.17:2.4: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, katattā ca akatattā ca evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.an2.17:2.5: Idha pana, brāhmaṇa, ekaccassa kāyasucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti kāyaduccaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.17:2.6: vacīsucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti vacīduccaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.17:2.7: manosucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti manoduccaritaṁ.
root_text.an2.17:2.8: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, katattā ca akatattā ca evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an2.17:3.1: “Abhikkantaṁ, bho gotama …pe… upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
root_text.an2.18:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an2.18:1.2: “ekaṁsenāhaṁ, ānanda, akaraṇīyaṁ vadāmi kāyaduccaritaṁ vacīduccaritaṁ manoduccaritan”ti.
root_text.an2.18:1.3: “Yamidaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā ekaṁsena akaraṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyaduccaritaṁ vacīduccaritaṁ manoduccaritaṁ tasmiṁ akaraṇīye kayiramāne ko ādīnavo pāṭikaṅkho”ti?
root_text.an2.18:1.4: “Yamidaṁ, ānanda, mayā ekaṁsena akaraṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyaduccaritaṁ vacīduccaritaṁ manoduccaritaṁ tasmiṁ akaraṇīye kayiramāne ayaṁ ādīnavo pāṭikaṅkho—
root_text.an2.18:1.9: kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an2.18:1.10: Yamidaṁ, ānanda, mayā ekaṁsena akaraṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyaduccaritaṁ vacīduccaritaṁ manoduccaritaṁ tasmiṁ akaraṇīye kayiramāne ayaṁ ādīnavo pāṭikaṅkho”ti.
root_text.an2.18:2.1: “Ekaṁsenāhaṁ, ānanda, karaṇīyaṁ vadāmi kāyasucaritaṁ vacīsucaritaṁ manosucaritan”ti.
root_text.an2.18:2.2: “Yamidaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā ekaṁsena karaṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyasucaritaṁ vacīsucaritaṁ manosucaritaṁ tasmiṁ karaṇīye kayiramāne ko ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho”ti?
root_text.an2.18:2.3: “Yamidaṁ, ānanda, mayā ekaṁsena karaṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyasucaritaṁ vacīsucaritaṁ manosucaritaṁ tasmiṁ karaṇīye kayiramāne ayaṁ ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho—
root_text.an2.18:2.8: kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an2.18:2.9: Yamidaṁ, ānanda, mayā ekaṁsena karaṇīyaṁ akkhātaṁ kāyasucaritaṁ vacīsucaritaṁ manosucaritaṁ tasmiṁ karaṇīye kayiramāne ayaṁ ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho”ti.
root_text.an2.19:1.3: No cedaṁ, bhikkhave, sakkā abhavissa akusalaṁ pajahituṁ, nāhaṁ evaṁ vadeyyaṁ:
root_text.an2.19:1.5: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, sakkā akusalaṁ pajahituṁ tasmāhaṁ evaṁ vadāmi:
root_text.an2.19:1.7: Akusalañca hidaṁ, bhikkhave, pahīnaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvatteyya nāhaṁ evaṁ vadeyyaṁ:
root_text.an2.19:1.9: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, akusalaṁ pahīnaṁ hitāya sukhāya saṁvattati tasmāhaṁ evaṁ vadāmi:
root_text.an2.19:2.3: No cedaṁ, bhikkhave, sakkā abhavissa kusalaṁ bhāvetuṁ, nāhaṁ evaṁ vadeyyaṁ:
root_text.an2.19:2.5: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, sakkā kusalaṁ bhāvetuṁ tasmāhaṁ evaṁ vadāmi:
root_text.an2.19:2.7: Kusalañca hidaṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvitaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya saṁvatteyya, nāhaṁ evaṁ vadeyyaṁ:
root_text.an2.19:2.9: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, kusalaṁ bhāvitaṁ hitāya sukhāya saṁvattati tasmāhaṁ evaṁ vadāmi:
root_text.an2.20:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, dhammā saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an2.20:1.4: Dunnikkhittassa, bhikkhave, padabyañjanassa atthopi dunnayo hoti.
root_text.an2.20:1.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā saddhammassa sammosāya antaradhānāya saṁvattantī”ti.
root_text.an2.20:2.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, dhammā saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattanti.
root_text.an2.20:2.4: Sunikkhittassa, bhikkhave, padabyañjanassa atthopi sunayo hoti.
root_text.an2.20:2.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā saddhammassa ṭhitiyā asammosāya anantaradhānāya saṁvattantī”ti.

translation_text.an2.11:1.3: The power of reflection and the power of development.
translation_text.an2.11:1.4: And what, mendicants, is the power of reflection?
translation_text.an2.11:1.6: ‘Bad conduct of body, speech, or mind has a bad, painful result in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an2.11:1.7: Reflecting like this, they give up bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and develop good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, keeping themselves pure.
translation_text.an2.11:1.8: This is called the power of reflection.
translation_text.an2.11:2.1: And what, mendicants, is the power of development?
translation_text.an2.11:2.2: In this context, the power of development is the power of the trainees.
translation_text.an2.11:2.3: For when you rely on the power of a trainee, you give up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an2.11:2.4: Then you don’t do anything unskillful, or practice anything bad.
translation_text.an2.11:2.5: This is called the power of development.
translation_text.an2.12:1.3: The power of reflection and the power of development.
translation_text.an2.12:1.4: And what, mendicants, is the power of reflection?
translation_text.an2.12:1.6: ‘Bad conduct of body, speech, or mind has a bad, painful result in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an2.12:1.7: Reflecting like this, they give up bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and develop good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, keeping themselves pure.
translation_text.an2.12:1.8: This is called the power of reflection.
translation_text.an2.12:2.1: And what, mendicants, is the power of development?
translation_text.an2.12:2.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness,
translation_text.an2.12:2.8: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.an2.12:2.9: This is called the power of development.
translation_text.an2.13:1.3: The power of reflection and the power of development.
translation_text.an2.13:1.4: And what, mendicants, is the power of reflection?
translation_text.an2.13:1.6: ‘Bad conduct of body, speech, or mind has a bad, painful result in both this life and the next.’
translation_text.an2.13:1.7: Reflecting like this, they give up bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and develop good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, keeping themselves pure.
translation_text.an2.13:1.8: This is called the power of reflection.
translation_text.an2.13:2.1: And what, mendicants, is the power of development?
translation_text.an2.13:2.2: It’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an2.13:2.3: As the placing of the mind and keeping it connected are stilled, they enter and remain in the second absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of immersion, with internal clarity and mind at one, without placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.an2.13:2.4: And with the fading away of rapture, they enter and remain in the third absorption, where they meditate with equanimity, mindful and aware, personally experiencing the bliss of which the noble ones declare, ‘Equanimous and mindful, one meditates in bliss.’
translation_text.an2.13:2.5: With the giving up of pleasure and pain and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, they enter and remain in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness.
translation_text.an2.13:2.6: This is called the power of development.
translation_text.an2.14:1.1: “Mendicants, the Realized One has two ways of teaching the Dhamma.
translation_text.an2.14:1.4: These are the Realized One’s two ways of teaching the Dhamma.”
translation_text.an2.15:1.1: “Mendicants, in a disciplinary issue, if neither the offending mendicant nor the accusing mendicant carefully checks themselves, you can expect that issue
translation_text.an2.15:1.2: will lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants won’t live comfortably.
translation_text.an2.15:1.3: But in a disciplinary issue, if both the offending mendicant and the accusing mendicant carefully check themselves, you can expect that issue
translation_text.an2.15:1.4: won’t lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants will live comfortably.
translation_text.an2.15:2.1: And how, mendicants, does an offending mendicant carefully check themselves?
translation_text.an2.15:2.3: ‘I have committed a certain unskillful offense with the body.
translation_text.an2.15:2.4: That mendicant saw me do this.
translation_text.an2.15:2.5: If I hadn’t committed that offense, they wouldn’t have seen me.
translation_text.an2.15:2.6: But since I did commit that offense, they did see me.
translation_text.an2.15:2.7: When they saw me, they were upset,
translation_text.an2.15:2.8: and they voiced their unhappiness to me.
translation_text.an2.15:2.11: So the mistake is mine alone, like someone who owes customs duty on their goods.’
translation_text.an2.15:2.12: That’s how, mendicants, an offending mendicant carefully checks themselves.
translation_text.an2.15:3.1: And how, mendicants, does an accusing mendicant carefully check themselves?
translation_text.an2.15:3.3: ‘This mendicant has committed a certain unskillful offense with the body.
translation_text.an2.15:3.5: If they hadn’t committed that offense, I wouldn’t have seen them.
translation_text.an2.15:3.6: But since they did commit that offense, I did see them.
translation_text.an2.15:3.7: When I saw them, I was upset,
translation_text.an2.15:3.8: and I voiced my unhappiness to them.
translation_text.an2.15:3.11: So the mistake is mine alone, like someone who owes customs duty on their goods.’
translation_text.an2.15:3.12: That’s how, mendicants, an accusing mendicant carefully checks themselves.
translation_text.an2.15:4.1: In a disciplinary issue, if neither the offending mendicant nor the accusing mendicant carefully checks themselves, you can expect that issue will lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants won’t live comfortably.
translation_text.an2.15:4.2: But in a disciplinary issue, if both the offending mendicant and the accusing mendicant carefully checks themselves, you can expect that issue won’t lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants will live comfortably.”
translation_text.an2.16:1.1: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an2.16:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an2.16:1.3: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell?”
translation_text.an2.16:1.4: “Unprincipled and immoral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.”
translation_text.an2.16:2.1: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm?”
translation_text.an2.16:2.2: “Principled and moral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.an2.16:3.2: As if he was righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, worthy Gotama has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.an2.16:3.3: I go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.an2.16:3.4: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
translation_text.an2.17:1.1: Then the brahmin Jānussoṇi went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an2.17:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an2.17:1.3: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell?”
translation_text.an2.17:1.4: “What they’ve done and what they’ve not done.
translation_text.an2.17:1.5: That’s why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.”
translation_text.an2.17:1.6: “But what is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm?”
translation_text.an2.17:1.7: “What they’ve done and what they’ve not done.
translation_text.an2.17:1.8: That’s why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.an2.17:1.9: “I don’t understand the meaning of what the worthy Gotama has said in brief, without explaining the details.
translation_text.an2.17:1.10: Worthy Gotama, please teach me this matter in detail so I can understand the meaning.”
translation_text.an2.17:1.11: “Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an2.17:2.1: “Firstly, brahmin, someone has done bad things and not done good things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an2.17:2.4: So what they’ve done and what they’ve not done is why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an2.17:2.5: Furthermore, brahmin, someone has done good things and not done bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an2.17:2.8: So what they’ve done and what they’ve not done is why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.an2.17:3.1: “Excellent, worthy Gotama! … From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
translation_text.an2.18:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an2.18:1.2: “Ānanda, I absolutely say that you should not do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.”
translation_text.an2.18:1.3: “But, sir, if someone does these things that should not be done, what drawbacks should they expect?”
translation_text.an2.18:1.6: After examination, sensible people criticize them.
translation_text.an2.18:1.8: They feel lost when they die.
translation_text.an2.18:1.9: And when their body breaks up, after death, they are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an2.18:1.10: These are the drawbacks they should expect.”
translation_text.an2.18:2.1: “Ānanda, I absolutely say that you should do good things by way of body, speech, and mind.”
translation_text.an2.18:2.2: “But, sir, if someone does these things that should be done, what benefits should they expect?”
translation_text.an2.18:2.5: After examination, sensible people praise them.
translation_text.an2.18:2.7: They don’t feel lost when they die.
translation_text.an2.18:2.8: And when the body breaks up, after death, they are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an2.18:2.9: These are the benefits they should expect.”
translation_text.an2.19:1.2: It is possible to give up the unskillful.
translation_text.an2.19:1.3: If it wasn’t possible, I wouldn’t say:
translation_text.an2.19:1.5: But it is possible, and so I say:
translation_text.an2.19:1.7: And if giving up the unskillful led to harm and suffering, I would not say:
translation_text.an2.19:1.9: But giving up the unskillful leads to welfare and happiness, so I say:
translation_text.an2.19:2.2: It is possible to develop the skillful.
translation_text.an2.19:2.3: If it wasn’t possible, I wouldn’t say:
translation_text.an2.19:2.5: But it is possible, and so I say:
translation_text.an2.19:2.7: If developing the skillful led to harm and suffering I wouldn’t say:
translation_text.an2.19:2.9: But developing the skillful leads to welfare and happiness, so I say:
translation_text.an2.20:1.1: “These two things, mendicants, lead to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an2.20:1.3: The words and phrases are misplaced, and the meaning is misinterpreted.
translation_text.an2.20:1.4: When the words and phrases are misplaced, the meaning is misinterpreted.
translation_text.an2.20:1.5: These two things lead to the decline and disappearance of the true teaching.
translation_text.an2.20:2.1: These two things lead to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.
translation_text.an2.20:2.3: The words and phrases are well organized, and the meaning is correctly interpreted.
translation_text.an2.20:2.4: When the words and phrases are well organized, the meaning is correctly interpreted.
translation_text.an2.20:2.5: These two things lead to the continuation, persistence, and enduring of the true teaching.”

variant_text.an2.11:2.2: yamidaṁ → yadidaṁ (si, sya-all) | sekhānametaṁ → sekhametaṁ (bj, sya-all, km); sekhānaṁ etaṁ (pts1ed)
variant_text.an2.15:1.2: na phāsuṁ → na phāsukaṁ (bj); na phāsu (mr) | viharissantī’ti → viharissanti (bj, sya-all, km, mr)
variant_text.an2.15:2.4: Maṁ so → tasmā maṁ so (sya-all); taṁ maṁ so (pts1ed)
variant_text.an2.19:1.3: cedaṁ → no ce taṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)

reference_text.an2.15:1.1: cck20.69, csp1ed15.56, csp2ed15.56, ms15A2_37, msdiv15, pts-vp-pli1.54.1, sya20.69, vri35.71
